description_long: This object is a cluster of elongated, narrow red flowers attached to a single greenish stem. The flowers are slender and slightly curved, resembling chili peppers or tubular blossoms, and are densely grouped at the top of the stem. The surface of the petals appears matte with a subtle texture, and the coloration is a deep, vibrant red with some natural variation. The stem is thin, green, and slightly woody, branching out to support the cluster of flowers. No distinct leaves are visible, and the overall structure is upright and compact. This type of flower could be found in urban parks, gardens, or as part of decorative landscaping.
description: A cluster of slender, curved red flowers on a single green stem, with a matte textured surface and no visible leaves.
description_view_0: The flower cluster is seen from the side, showing the dense grouping of red, tubular petals and the upright green stem.
description_view_1: A more frontal view reveals the spread and curvature of the red petals, with the cluster appearing fuller and more symmetrical.
description_view_2: This view highlights the overlapping arrangement of the petals, with the stem partially obscured by the dense flower cluster.
description_view_3: The flower cluster is viewed from the opposite side, showing the outward splay of the petals and the branching at the top of the stem.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a dense, somewhat spiral cluster at the terminal end of the stem, with overlapping and slightly twisted orientation, lacking clear radial or bilateral symmetry.
